a:r.a:t: Different tasks need different execution strategies, and this slide shows the four main categories. Basic mode is a single LLM call with no tools -- used for greetings, simple Q and A, and general knowledge questions. It is fast, cheap, and the router defaults to it when confidence is low. ReAct mode is the full reasoning loop with tool calling -- the agent can call any of its allowed tools, evaluate results, and loop up to its max_steps limit. This is used by the general agent, data_analyst, utility, and graph_manager. RAG mode is a specialized pipeline specifically designed for document search: it retrieves evidence from indexed documents, grades relevance with a judge LLM, synthesizes a grounded answer, and produces structured citations. The rag_worker agent uses this mode exclusively. Coordinator mode is the most powerful -- it does not do work itself but instead decomposes tasks using a planner agent, dispatches subtasks to parallel workers (like data_analyst, rag_worker, utility), collects their results through a team mailbox system, synthesizes with a finalizer, and optionally fact-checks with a verifier. The coordinator can manage up to 4 concurrent workers and will loop through revision rounds if the verifier flags issues. Each agent in our system declares exactly one mode in its definition file.
